rationale: Lexend: designed by Bonnie Shaver-Troup et al. specifically to improve reading proficiency. Pairing it with clinical/oncology copy signals 'we care about how this is read'.
ClashGrotesk: contemporary geometric grotesk with humanist warmth in the lowercase a/g/e. Chosen over Inter/Söhne/Helvetica to avoid both 'tech-startup' and 'corporate-pharma' tropes.
Both faces are LIGHT-weight defaults, an anti-pharma move — pharma marketing defaults to semibold/bold for legal-confidence; Lunit goes the opposite way.
